description: Reference registry and experimental benchmark targets for the v4.0 multi-technology engines (silicon, organic, tandem). Every benchmark and model parameterization cites a peer-reviewed source with DOI. 'verified' = the citation (title/authors/journal/year and, where marked, the DOI string) was cross-checked against the Consensus academic index and/or the publisher page during the v4.0 build; MEDIUM entries carry canonical, widely-cited DOIs that were not independently re-fetched in that pass.

richter_2012_prb.citation: A. Richter, S. W. Glunz, F. Werner, J. Schmidt, A. Cuevas, 'Improved quantitative description of Auger recombination in crystalline silicon', Phys. Rev. B 86, 165202 (2012)
richter_2012_prb.used_for: Intrinsic Auger + radiative recombination parameterization in physics/silicon.py

niewelt_2022_solmat.citation: T. Niewelt et al., 'Reassessment of the intrinsic bulk recombination in crystalline silicon', Sol. Energy Mater. Sol. Cells 235, 111467 (2022)
niewelt_2022_solmat.used_for: Alternative intrinsic recombination model (auger_model='niewelt2022'); 29.4% c-Si single-junction limit

kerr_2002_jap.citation: M. J. Kerr, A. Cuevas, 'General parameterization of Auger recombination in crystalline silicon', J. Appl. Phys. 91, 2473 (2002)
kerr_2002_jap.used_for: Implied-Voc / lifetime methodology; historical Auger parameterization

green_2008_solmat.citation: M. A. Green, 'Self-consistent optical parameters of intrinsic silicon at 300 K including temperature coefficients', Sol. Energy Mater. Sol. Cells 92, 1305 (2008)
green_2008_solmat.used_for: c-Si absorption coefficient table alpha(lambda) in physics/silicon.py

tiedje_1984_ted.citation: T. Tiedje, E. Yablonovitch, G. D. Cody, B. G. Brooks, 'Limiting efficiency of silicon solar cells', IEEE Trans. Electron Devices 31, 711 (1984)
tiedje_1984_ted.used_for: Lambertian light-trapping absorptance A = alpha/(alpha + 1/(4 n^2 W))

altermatt_2003_jap.citation: P. P. Altermatt, A. Schenk, F. Geelhaar, G. Heiser, 'Reassessment of the intrinsic carrier density in crystalline silicon in view of band-gap narrowing', J. Appl. Phys. 93, 1598 (2003)

green_1981_sse.citation: M. A. Green, 'Solar cell fill factors: General graph and empirical expressions', Solid-State Electron. 24, 788 (1981)

yoshikawa_2017_natenergy.citation: K. Yoshikawa et al., 'Silicon heterojunction solar cell with interdigitated back contacts for a photoconversion efficiency over 26%', Nat. Energy 2, 17032 (2017)
yoshikawa_2017_natenergy.used_for: SHJ-IBC preset benchmark (26.3%; improved to 26.6-26.7% in the companion paper)

yoshikawa_2017_solmat.citation: K. Yoshikawa et al., 'Exceeding conversion efficiency of 26% by heterojunction interdigitated back contact solar cell with thin film Si technology', Sol. Energy Mater. Sol. Cells 173, 37 (2017)

lin_2023_natenergy.citation: H. Lin et al., 'Silicon heterojunction solar cells with up to 26.81% efficiency achieved by electrically optimized nanocrystalline-silicon hole contact layers', Nat. Energy 8, 789 (2023)
lin_2023_natenergy.used_for: SHJ both-side preset benchmark (26.81%, Voc 751.4 mV, FF up to 86.59%)

richter_2021_natenergy.citation: A. Richter et al., 'Design rules for high-efficiency both-sides-contacted silicon solar cells with balanced charge carrier transport and recombination losses', Nat. Energy 6, 429 (2021)

wang_2024_natcomm.citation: G. Wang et al., '27.09%-efficiency silicon heterojunction back contact solar cell and going beyond', Nat. Commun. 15 (2024)
wang_2024_natcomm.used_for: Context: current certified HBC frontier for c-Si

green_2021_tables57.citation: M. A. Green, E. D. Dunlop, J. Hohl-Ebinger, M. Yoshita, N. Kopidakis, X. Hao, 'Solar cell efficiency tables (version 57)', Prog. Photovolt. Res. Appl. 29, 3 (2021)
green_2021_tables57.used_for: PERC (24.06% LONGi record class) and Al-BSF representative presets; cross-technology record context

yuan_2019_joule.citation: J. Yuan et al., 'Single-Junction Organic Solar Cell with over 15% Efficiency Using Fused-Ring Acceptor with Electron-Deficient Core', Joule 3, 1140 (2019)
yuan_2019_joule.used_for: PM6:Y6 preset benchmark (15.7%; Voc 0.83 V, Jsc 25.3 mA/cm2, FF 74.8%) and 300 nm thickness roll-off (13.6%)

li_2021_natenergy.citation: C. Li et al., 'Non-fullerene acceptors with branched side chains and improved molecular packing to exceed 18% efficiency in organic solar cells', Nat. Energy 6, 605 (2021)

cui_2021_advmater.citation: Y. Cui et al., 'Single-Junction Organic Photovoltaic Cell with 19% Efficiency', Adv. Mater. 33, 2102420 (2021)

li_2025_natmater.citation: C. Li et al., 'Non-fullerene acceptors with high crystallinity and photoluminescence quantum yield enable >20% efficiency organic solar cells', Nat. Mater. 24 (2025)
li_2025_natmater.used_for: PM6:L8-BO-C4 preset benchmark (20.42%, certified 20.1%; Voc 0.894 V, FF 81.6%)

koster_2005_prb.citation: L. J. A. Koster, E. C. P. Smits, V. D. Mihailetchi, P. W. M. Blom, 'Device model for the operation of polymer/fullerene bulk heterojunction solar cells', Phys. Rev. B 72, 085205 (2005)
koster_2005_prb.used_for: BHJ drift-collection / recombination competition picture; P3HT:PCBM baseline

scharber_2006_advmater.citation: Scharber, M. C. et al., Design Rules for Donors in Bulk-Heterojunction Solar Cells - Towards 10% Energy-Conversion Efficiency, Advanced Materials 18, 789-794 (2006)
scharber_2006_advmater.used_for: Estimation model for NON-certified donor:acceptor pairs in the organic blend builder: Voc = (|HOMO_D| - |LUMO_A|)/e - 0.3 V with conservative EQE/FF assumptions; estimates always flagged EXTRAPOLATED

alashouri_2020_science.citation: A. Al-Ashouri et al., 'Monolithic perovskite/silicon tandem solar cell with >29% efficiency by enhanced hole extraction', Science 370, 1300 (2020)
alashouri_2020_science.used_for: 2020-grade tandem benchmark (29.15% certified; Voc 1.92 V, 1.68 eV top cell)

chin_2023_science.citation: X. Y. Chin et al., 'Interface passivation for 31.25%-efficient perovskite/silicon tandem solar cells', Science 381, 59 (2023)

mariotti_2023_science.citation: S. Mariotti et al., 'Interface engineering for high-performance, triple-halide perovskite-silicon tandem solar cells', Science 381, 63 (2023)
mariotti_2023_science.used_for: 2023-grade tandem benchmark upper bound (32.5% certified, Voc 2.00 V)

jia_2025_nature.citation: L. Jia et al., 'Efficient perovskite/silicon tandem with asymmetric self-assembly molecule', Nature 644, 912 (2025)
jia_2025_nature.used_for: 2025-grade tandem benchmark (34.58% certified, LONGi/Soochow; Voc ~2 V)

he_2023_nature.citation: R. He et al., 'Improving interface quality for 1-cm2 all-perovskite tandem solar cells', Nature 618, 80 (2023)
he_2023_nature.used_for: All-perovskite tandem context (27.0%, certified 26.4%, Voc 2.12 V)

masuko_2014_jpv.citation: K. Masuko et al., 'Achievement of More Than 25% Conversion Efficiency With Crystalline Silicon Heterojunction Solar Cell', IEEE J. Photovolt. 4, 1433 (2014)

shockley_1961_jap.citation: W. Shockley, H. J. Queisser, 'Detailed Balance Limit of Efficiency of p-n Junction Solar Cells', J. Appl. Phys. 32, 510 (1961)

pettersson_1999_jap.citation: L. A. A. Pettersson, L. S. Roman, O. Inganäs, 'Modeling photocurrent action spectra of photovoltaic devices based on organic thin films', J. Appl. Phys. 86, 487 (1999)

burkhard_2010_advmater.citation: G. F. Burkhard, E. T. Hoke, M. D. McGehee, 'Accounting for Interference, Scattering, and Electrode Absorption to Make Accurate Internal Quantum Efficiency Measurements in Organic and Other Thin Solar Cells', Adv. Mater. 22, 3293 (2010)
burkhard_2010_advmater.used_for: TMM implementation style (Stanford/McGehee script); ITO/PEDOT optical constants

sievers_2006_jap.citation: D. W. Sievers, V. Shrotriya, Y. Yang, 'Modeling optical effects and thickness dependent current in polymer bulk-heterojunction solar cells', J. Appl. Phys. 100, 114509 (2006)
sievers_2006_jap.used_for: Published oscillatory Jsc(L) benchmark for P3HT-class blends

kotlarski_2008_jap.citation: J. D. Kotlarski, P. W. M. Blom, L. J. A. Koster, M. Lenes, L. H. Slooff, 'Combined optical and electrical modeling of polymer:fullerene bulk heterojunction solar cells', J. Appl. Phys. 103, 084502 (2008)
kotlarski_2008_jap.used_for: Coupled optical-electrical benchmark; interference above 250 nm thickness

monestier_2007_solmat.citation: F. Monestier et al., 'Modeling the short-circuit current density of polymer solar cells based on P3HT:PCBM blend', Sol. Energy Mater. Sol. Cells 91, 405 (2007)
monestier_2007_solmat.used_for: P3HT:PCBM complex refractive index; Jsc(L) interference benchmark

rosa_2021_solener.citation: E. H. S. Rosa et al., 'Simulation of organic solar cells power conversion efficiency', Sol. Energy 221 (2021)
rosa_2021_solener.used_for: Published TMM-vs-experiment accuracy band (~16% average) contextualizing this tool's <=5% deviations

im_2023_molecules.citation: C. Im et al., 'Spatial Balance of Photogenerated Charge Carriers in Active Layers of Polymer Solar Cells', Molecules 28, 2985 (2023)
im_2023_molecules.used_for: NFA-blend TMM thickness study: PCE maximum near ~100 nm

kerremans_2020_aom.citation: R. Kerremans et al., 'The Optical Constants of Solution-Processed Semiconductors — New Challenges with Perovskites and Non-Fullerene Acceptors', Adv. Opt. Mater. 8, 2000319 (2020)

rakic_1995_ao.citation: A. D. Rakic, 'Algorithm for the determination of intrinsic optical constants of metal films: application to aluminum', Appl. Opt. 34, 4755 (1995)

johnson_1972_prb.citation: P. B. Johnson, R. W. Christy, 'Optical Constants of the Noble Metals', Phys. Rev. B 6, 4370 (1972)

burgelman_2000_tsf.citation: M. Burgelman, P. Nollet, S. Degrave, 'Modelling polycrystalline semiconductor solar cells', Thin Solid Films 361-362, 527 (2000)
burgelman_2000_tsf.used_for: SCAPS-1D — the incumbent this tool imports from / exports to

sahli_2018_natmater.citation: Sahli, F. et al., Fully textured monolithic perovskite/silicon tandem solar cells with 25.2% power conversion efficiency, Nature Materials 17, 820-826 (2018)
sahli_2018_natmater.used_for: nc-Si:H recombination-junction interconnect option in the tandem layer library (textured-compatible junction; certified 25.2% device)

richardson_2016_ees.citation: Richardson, G. et al., Can slow-moving ions explain hysteresis in the current-voltage curves of perovskite solar cells?, Energy & Environmental Science 9, 1476-1485 (2016)
richardson_2016_ees.used_for: Reduced-order scan-rate hysteresis model (utils/stability.py): ionic-drift origin of hysteresis, vanishing at fast and slow scan-rate limits with a peak at intermediate rates

khenkin_2020_natenergy.citation: Khenkin, M. V. et al., Consensus statement for stability assessment and reporting for perovskite photovoltaics based on ISOS procedures, Nature Energy 5, 35-49 (2020)
khenkin_2020_natenergy.used_for: T80 lifetime metric and ISOS stability-protocol conventions behind the Arrhenius lifetime-projection tool (utils/stability.py)

skoplaki_2009_solen.citation: Skoplaki, E. & Palyvos, J. A., On the temperature dependence of photovoltaic module electrical performance: A review of efficiency/power correlations, Solar Energy 83, 614-624 (2009)
skoplaki_2009_solen.used_for: NOCT cell-temperature model and linear power-temperature correlation class used by the all-technology energy-yield estimator (utils/energy_yield.py)

steiner_2012_apl.citation: Steiner, M. A. & Geisz, J. F., Non-linear luminescent coupling in series-connected multijunction solar cells, Applied Physics Letters 101, 073912 (2012)
steiner_2012_apl.used_for: Luminescent-coupling formulation in physics/tandem.py: coupled current proportional to the luminescent junction's excess recombination (J_gen - J)

jager_2020_solrrl.citation: Jaeger, K. et al., Perovskite/Silicon Tandem Solar Cells: Effect of Luminescent Coupling and Bifaciality, Solar RRL 5, 2000628 (2020)
jager_2020_solrrl.used_for: Magnitude anchor for tandem luminescent coupling (>50% of excess top-cell pairs usable by the silicon bottom cell) and energy-yield-with-LC context

jain_2004_solmat.citation: Jain, A. & Kapoor, A., Exact analytical solutions of the parameters of real solar cells using Lambert W-function, Solar Energy Materials and Solar Cells 81, 269-277 (2004)
jain_2004_solmat.used_for: Exact Lambert-W closed form of the single-diode J-V model used by the measurement-fitting workflow (utils/measurement_fit.py)
jain_2004_solmat.note: Canonical, widely cited paper; DOI from publisher record. Flagged for Consensus cross-check in the next audited build.

organic.note: thickness roll-off check; analytical collection model, wider tolerance
processing-dependent legacy system; representative mid-range value

tandem.note: PCE and Voc reproduced closely; the analytical engine trades ~7% low Jsc against ~6% high FF (documented in VALIDATION_MULTI_TECH.md)
